sanskrit: भवान् भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः। अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च।।
transliteration: bhavān bhīṣmaś ca karṇaś ca kṛpaś ca samitiñjayaḥ / aśvatthāmā vikarṇaś ca saumadattis tathaiva ca //
literal_meaning: Yourself, Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Kṛpa—always victorious in battle—Aśvatthāmā, Vikarṇa, and likewise the son of Somadatta.
contextual_meaning: Duryodhana bolsters his fragile self-worth by cataloguing his invincible champions, projecting an illusion of omnipotent allies onto the battlefield of his mind; this is the ego’s desperate attempt to deny inner powerlessness by clinging to external figures who promise never to lose. Psychologically it mirrors the moment when a person facing life-altering conflict inflates the perceived strength of their support network to fend off the dread of facing consequences alone.
psychological_state: A leader outwardly boasting an unbreakable team while inwardly trembling at the possibility that none of them can ultimately protect him from personal annihilation.
trigger_scenario: A manager about to present a risky project to the board, reciting in their head the list of star employees on their side to calm the creeping fear that the plan could still fail and expose their inadequacy.
anchor_text: True strength is inside you, not in the names you list.
